name: Commander.js (CLI Argument Parsing)

performance.notes: Startup time only; no runtime overhead after parsing

agent_readiness.notes: The standard Node.js CLI argument parsing library. program.option(), program.command(), program.argument(). Auto-generated help text. Command nesting for subcommands. Required/optional arguments. Type coercion (int, float, bool). Default values. Version flag (-V). TypeScript types. Used by ESLint, Webpack, and countless CLI tools. Simple for basic CLIs, powerful for complex multi-command CLIs. Yargs is alternative with more features.

gotchas: Global program state can be awkward for complex CLIs
Async command handlers require careful error handling
API mutates global state by default
best_when: Node.js CLI tools with commands and options
Teams wanting simple, widely-used CLI framework
Node.js CLIs distributed as npm packages
avoid_when: Complex validation (Yargs has more built-in validation)
summary: Commander.js is the standard Node.js CLI argument parsing library. Simple API: program.option(), program.command(), program.argument(). Auto-generated help text. Nested subcommands. TypeScript types. Used by ESLint, Webpack, and hundreds of popular CLI tools. Best for straightforward to medium-complexity CLIs. Yargs offers more built-in features for complex CLIs.
